entry.title: Washington Post: 17 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia set to renew ground assaults, analysts say, as strikes pelt east
  • Ukrainian cargo plane carrying mines crashes in Greece, officials say
  • Wild boars that roam Rome must be killed, officials say
  • An attack on a military base in Somalia shows al-Shabab’s deadly power
  • Outcry over U.S. visa delays after Kenyan sprinter nearly misses race
  • Russian forces ordered to intensify attacks across Ukraine
  • Mexico arrests Caro Quintero, drug lord convicted in death of DEA agent
  • Francisco Morales Bermudez, ex-Peruvian military ruler, dies at 100
  • Biden vows expanded U.S. role in Mideast as controversial trip ends
  • Md. churches’ youth group ‘safe,’ leaving Panama amid political unrest
  • 90 days in Russian prison: A Ukrainian teen’s tale of terror and hope
  • White House says Russian officials visited Iran twice to examine drones
  • 'Those people': French minister's LGBTQ remarks spark anger
  • UAE sentences former Khashoggi lawyer to 3 years in prison
  • Russia strikes south Ukraine city, presses attacks in east
  • Fires scorch France, Spain; temperature-related deaths soar
  • China floods leave at least 12 dead, thousands evacuated
  • Holocaust survivors mark 80 years since mass Paris roundup
  • Zimbabwe students scoop international awards for moot court
  • 'I worry about this place': A day in Ukraine's Donetsk
  • Experts to comb site of plane crash in northern Greece
  • Huasos reciben bendición en Santuario Nacional de Maipu
  • Workers are picking up extra jobs just to pay for gas and food
  • Soaring dollar could help Fed in fight against inflation
  • If We’re Lucky, OPEC Is Wrong About Oil Demand Going Up
  • Nobody Knows How Long Inflation Will Last. That’s Life.
  • Draghi Has Entrenched His Influence Even If Coalition Falls
  • It’s Not Too Late for Princess Diana to Save Britain’s Monarchy
  • How China Is Opening Up Further to Global Investors
  • Iran Has Brought Israel and the Arab States Closer. Is Saudi Arabia Next?
  • Why US Sees Saudi Arabia Again as Partner, Not Pariah
  • Cut Biden some slack. U.S. presidents have to deal with dictators.
  • Presto! Manchin’s party of one makes the Democrats’ program disappear.
  • How to turn Youngkin’s misstep on same-sex marriage into an opportunity
  • Make birth control more accessible
  • At long last, the Olympic injustice done to Jim Thorpe is corrected
  • A question-spangled banner
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 17 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 17 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-17 11:21AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Stricter bail conditions to apply to offenders under Hong Kong’s future national security law, justice minister says
  • ‘I was forced to accept the increase’: frustration, concern as fare adjustments for taxi rides in Hong Kong kick in
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong health experts ‘likely to approve vaccines for children aged below 3 next month’, as city logs 3,486 cases
  • Surge in Hong Kong teachers quitting leads to hiring difficulties for schools for certain subjects, education minister says
  • Hong Kong hires officer in Europe to attract family offices to invest in the city, Greater Bay Area, official says
  • Utility bills, hospital visit records can be used as proof of residency in Hong Kong to qualify for HK$5,000 voucher scheme, finance chief says
  • Five areas where John Lee can improve the lives of Hong Kong youth
  • No smartphone, no entry? Isolated by Covid-19 rules, Hong Kong’s elderly dread changes to ‘Leave Home Safe’ mobile app
  • Hong Kong’s prosecutors will not bow to intimidation amid threat of US sanctions
  • Patriotic Education Centre to launch classes for Hong Kong students from September
  • Woman arrested for allegedly duping 22 residents out of HK$130,000 by offering fake discounted tickets to Hong Kong Disneyland
  • Douyin, China’s TikTok, tests food delivery, bringing ByteDance into a market dominated by Meituan and Alibaba
  • China and EU to hold high-level trade talks this week after months of delays
  • Chinese scientists say lab-grown meatballs are ready for mass production
  • Dior draws fresh fire in China, this time over ‘hallmark’ skirt resembling Ming and Qing attire
  • Sri Lanka turmoil highlights risk for Chinese firms investing overseas
  • Military drills a sign of Beijing’s increasingly aggressive stance towards Taiwan, analysts say
  • Ending Australia coal ban would put China ties back on track, minister says
  • Chinese diplomats flock to Africa in response to Western charm offensive
  • Chinese foreign minister criticises US ‘meddling’ in Middle East as Joe Biden continues tour of region
  • Scientists urge people to take second Covid vaccine booster if offered as Omicron continues to spread
  • Malaysia 1MDB scandal: Government rejected Jho Low’s US$337 million offer to settle case, report says
  • Malaysians throng Japanese Bon Odori dance festival, defy Muslim groups’ stay away call
  • Was Indonesian freedom fighter Kapitan Pattimura actually a Muslim? Conspiracy highlights rising historical revisionism
  • Marcos misinterprets the cause of the Philippines’ food crisis. It was decentralisation, not weak leadership
  • As Malaysia’s parliament resumes, will it be Najib Razak’s last meeting?
  • Thailand lures wealthy foreigners with land ownership offer
  • G20 in Indonesia agrees on food security, but war prevents formal communique
  • Malaysian assets at risk globally as heirs of sultan, who are Philippine nationals, claim US$15 billion award
  • Russia strikes strategic south Ukrainian city as it eyes all of the country’s east
  • All 8 Ukrainian crew members killed in cargo plane crash in Greece
  • ‘Ready for Rishi’: Why Indian netizens can’t stop talking about Rishi Sunak’s chances in the UK’s PM race
  • War-torn Syrian town springs back to life with Jackie Chan-produced film shoot
  • Will Biden’s fist-bump with Saudi crown prince MBS dent US president’s image as human rights defender?
  • Britain’s Duchess Camilla celebrates milestone birthday – future ‘Queen Consort’ is 75
  • Biden ends trip with US-Saudi relations on the mend but few other wins
  • Ukraine athlete favourite to win gold at world championships
  • Hundreds die as heatwave grips Europe and temperatures soar above 40ºC
  • US: 6 dead in Montana 21-car pile up after windy dust storm with ‘zero visibility’
  • ‘No vacuum for China, Russia, Iran’ to fill in Middle East, says Biden at Arab summit
  • Amid economic slowdown, green bonds can help Southeast Asia stay on road to net zero in climate fight
  • Climate change a far greater threat to global economy than Covid-19 pandemic amid rising intensity of heatwaves and floods, Swiss Re says
  • Hong Kong hotelier hopes for restriction-free travel as industry slowly returns to pre-pandemic heyday across the world
  • Decolonising culture: Paul Gauguin’s Javanese ‘mistress’ Annah and ‘Malay girl’ in a W. Somerset Maugham short story given voices in novellas of Mirandi Riwoe
  • Love Netflix’s Persuasion? 5 movie locations you can visit, from a picturesque seaside town to grand houses
  • Roller skating entrepreneur talks about finding her tribe in Hong Kong, after growing up in South Africa and being a safari guide
  • The best ice cream in Hong Kong and its little-known history in the city
  • Why are there so many artisan coffee shops opening in Hong Kong? How city’s drinking habits have evolved
  • Hong Kong ‘demon chef’ Alvin Leung on food art, life and death, heaven and hell, and his new menu The Masterpieces
  • Best mountain running and climbing films free on YouTube from Touching The Void to Project Endless
  • Likened to Hollywood hit Love Story, this film beat Jackie Chan, Jet Li at the Hong Kong box office in 1993
  • Meet Prince Philip of Serbia, the bitcoin buff: the American-born son of Alexander, crown prince of Yugoslavia, once worked at The Ritz London and is a vocal crypto advocate
  • Meet Anastasia ‘Stassie’ Karanikolaou, Kylie Jenner’s best friend: she’s a fashion influencer who’s modelled for Rihanna’s Savage x Fenty … but what if that Kardashian fan event never happened?
  • Who is Huma Abedin, Bradley Cooper’s rumoured new romance? She’s Hillary Clinton’s right-hand woman, an author, and was married to disgraced US politician Anthony Weiner
  • 16 richest actors in Marvel’s Thor franchise – net worths, ranked: from Chris Hemsworth and Natalie Portman, of course, to Christian Bale and Scarlett Johansson, but who tops the list?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.content:

  • Rubina Pabani and Poppy Jay: ‘We’re not sexperts – we’re sex clowns’

  • Documenta 15: Germany art exhibition chief resigns amid outrage over anti-Semitic works

  • Leftover makeup is given new life as paint thanks to SminkArt
  • EDITORIAL: Only radical reform will keep JCP politically relevant
  • VOX POPULI: July 16, the 77th anniversary of the world’s first atomic test
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 17 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
